alternatives: Keep callback queues and informal thread documentation as the concurrency model.
Put all asynchronous code on MainActor.
Use unstructured or detached tasks by default.
consequences: Actor ownership and data crossings become explicit and reviewable.
Some boundary models must become immutable Sendable snapshots.
Task lifetime and cancellation become part of feature design instead of cleanup after defects.
Legacy adapters require deliberate terminal-state and cancellation handling.
UIKit and SwiftUI presentation mutation share one MainActor contract.
context: Swift 6 makes previously implicit concurrency assumptions visible. A five-year iOS standard needs one model for isolation, Sendable crossings, task lifetime, cancellation, UI mutation, continuations, and legacy callbacks without prescribing one business architecture or hiding risk behind unchecked escape hatches.
decision: Adopt Swift 6 complete strict concurrency, assign every mutable state boundary an explicit actor or synchronization owner, require Sendable values across isolation boundaries, prefer structured task ownership, propagate cancellation, isolate UI presentation on MainActor, and adapt one-shot legacy callbacks through exactly-once checked continuations.
migration: Enable Swift 6 complete strict-concurrency diagnostics incrementally by target.
Assign ownership to mutable shared state and isolate UI-facing mutation on MainActor.
Replace cross-boundary reference sharing with Sendable values or actor methods.
Convert fire-and-forget work to structured or explicitly owned tasks and add cancellation cleanup.
Wrap remaining callback/delegate boundaries in checked adapters, documenting temporary unchecked conformances with owners and removal dates.
title: Concurrency and Isolation
verification_impact: The single final joined AI consistency review checks isolation, Sendable crossings, task ownership, cancellation, exactly-once continuation semantics, legacy bridges, and MainActor presentation consistency across the complete Standards change.
